summary_en: ROIC 4.3% target by FY3/27 (WACC baseline 4%, cost-of-equity 7%). Mid-term plan: ¥10.0B sales, 5% operating margin. 5-year capital allocation: growth investment ¥35–50B (healthcare focus), shareholder returns ¥5–6B, policy-stock sales ¥2–3B, operating cash generation ¥40–50B. Current ROE 3.2%, PBR 0.8x.
title_en: Measures toward Realizing Management Conscious of Cost of Capital and Share Price (Update)
translation_en_brief:
Asahi Rubber filed an update to its capital-cost and shareholder-value strategy on 26 May 2026, following Tokyo Stock Exchange guidance. The company disclosed that current ROE stands at 3.2% and PBR at 0.8x, with ROIC insufficient to meet shareholder expectations. Structural issues cited include over-reliance on automotive OEM supply (>50% of sales), declining LED-lighting revenue from automotive interiors, and low business-expansion capability due to in-house bias.

The company has set a midterm target of ¥10.0B consolidated sales and 5% operating margin by the end of FY3/27, which would deliver ROIC of 4.3%—exceeding its fixed WACC baseline of 4% (equity cost-of-capital 7%). Over the next five years, Asahi will deploy ¥35–50B in growth investment (focused on wellness and healthcare), ¥5–6B in shareholder returns (incremental dividends), and generate ¥40–50B in operating cash flow. The company will fund this through sequential sale of policy stocks (¥2–3B), disciplined capital-structure optimization, and ODM (original-design manufacturing) transition to raise margin.
